brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +58.6% since Feb 27 — Brent $112.57 (Fri close); Houthi third front + Bab al-Mandeb threat; Islamabad quad summit convenes; strongest monthly gain since Nov 1973
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 27 (Fri close): Brent $112.57/bbl, +4.22%. Markets closed Mar 28-29 (weekend). Houthi missile barrage at Israel opens third front — Bab al-Mandeb closure threatened — dual chokepoint scenario. Islamabad quadrilateral summit (Pakistan/Saudi/Turkey/Egypt) convening. Goldman: $14-18/bbl geopolitical risk premium. Brent-WTI spread $12.93. IRGC university ultimatum expires noon Mar 30. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +49.8% since Feb 27 — WTI $99.64 (Fri close); intraday crossed $100; Islamabad summit convenes; IRGC ultimatum expires noon Mar 30
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 27 (Fri close): WTI $99.64, +5.46%. Markets closed Mar 28-29 (weekend). Intraday high $100.04 — briefly crossed $100 for first time. Gas at $3.98/gal national average (AAA). Brent-WTI spread $12.93. Dow in correction. VIX 31.05. Islamabad quad summit convening. IRGC ultimatum on university campuses expires noon Mar 30. Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.00 / +33.6% since Feb 27 — $3.98 AAA avg — approaching $4 threshold — highest since Aug 2022 — EIA: $4.096/gal week ending Mar 23
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). AAA Mar 28: $3.98/gal (+33.6% since war began). EIA weekly data: $4.096/gal for week ending Mar 23. Up $1.00 in one month — approaching $4 psychological threshold — highest since August 2022. Source: AAA / EIA.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -510 pts / -7.4% — S&P 6,369 (seven-month low); Dow correction; VIX 31.05; ~50% chance Fed rate hike by Dec; CNN 'extreme fear'
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Mar 27 (Fri close): 6,368.85, -1.67% — seven-month low. Fifth consecutive weekly decline — longest since 2022. Dow 45,167 entered correction territory. Nasdaq 20,948 (~13% below record). VIX 31.05 — first close above 30 since Mar 2025. Markets now price ~50% chance of Fed rate hike by December — dramatic reversal from cut expectations. 10-Year Treasury at 4.44%. Energy only positive S&P sector since war. XOM +3.36% to $170.99. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNN.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — 11,000+ targets — 850+ Tomahawks — 66% of missile/drone production destroyed — Houthi third front — IRGC ultimatum — $200B supplemental
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 30: ~$1.0-1.5B/day. CENTCOM: 11,000+ targets struck, 66% of Iranian missile/drone/naval production destroyed. 850+ Tomahawks expended. 82nd Airborne deploying. Houthi third front active. IRGC university ultimatum expires noon Mar 30. Islamabad quad summit convening. Pentagon $200B supplemental faces bipartisan resistance. Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct military, $50-210B economic. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / Penn Wharton.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (30 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1-1.5B/day ongoing — ~$35.5B — $200B supplemental faces bipartisan resistance
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 30: ~$35.5B. Pentagon $200B supplemental at ~$1B/day — faces significant bipartisan resistance. Sen. Gallego: figure implies 'a long war.' National debt at $39T. Rubio: objectives completed in 'next couple weeks.' Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct military, $50-210B economic. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / The Hill / Penn Wharton.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 11,000+ US targets — 66% of missile/drone/naval production destroyed — 150+ vessels sunk — 850+ Tomahawks — Arak heavy water + Ardakan yellowcake struck
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM (Mar 29): 11,000+ US targets struck, 66% of Iranian missile, drone, and naval production facilities destroyed. 8,000+ combat flights. 850+ Tomahawks expended. 150+ Iranian vessels sunk. Israel struck Shahid Khondab Heavy Water Complex (Arak) and Ardakan yellowcake plant. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~30,500+ through Day 30. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Axios / DoD.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 US KIA total — 7 enemy fire + 6 KC-135 crash — 300+ WIA — 15+ wounded at Prince Sultan AB (Mar 27) — air raid sirens in Bahrain/Kuwait (Mar 29)
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 13 US KIA total: 6 in Kuwait drone attack (Mar 1), 1 from injuries in Saudi strike (Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26), 6 in KC-135 crash in western Iraq (Mar 12, non-hostile). 300+ wounded per CENTCOM. 15+ US service members wounded (5 seriously) at Prince Sultan AB, Saudi Arabia from 6 ballistic missiles + 29 drones (Mar 27). Air raid sirens in Bahrain and Kuwait (Mar 29). 82nd Airborne deploying. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 300+ total per CENTCOM — 15+ wounded at Prince Sultan AB (5 seriously) — air raid sirens Bahrain/Kuwait Mar 29 — 50,000+ deployed
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. 300+ WIA per CENTCOM. 15+ US service members wounded (5 seriously) at Prince Sultan Air Base from 6 ballistic missiles + 29 drones (Mar 27). Air raid sirens activated in Bahrain and Kuwait (Mar 29). 50,000+ US troops in CENTCOM AOR. 82nd Airborne (1st BCT + HQ + enablers) deploying. Trump weighing 10,000 additional combat troops. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Stars and Stripes.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Red Crescent: 1,900+ — HRANA: 3,461 — Health Ministry: 23,000+ total casualties — 244 women, 231 children among dead — wide discrepancy
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources. Iranian Red Crescent: ~1,900 killed. HRANA (US-based): 3,461 documented. Health Ministry: 23,000+ total casualties (incl. wounded). 244 women and 231 children among the dead per Health Ministry. Independent verification severely hampered by internet shutdowns. Estimated aggregate including military: ~6,800. 500+ ballistic/naval missiles and ~2,000 drones fired by Iran. Source: Iranian Red Crescent / HRANA / Health Ministry / WaPo.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,189 killed (124 children, 42 health workers) — 3,229 injured — ~1M displaced — IDF: 800+ Hezbollah killed — 3 journalists + 9 paramedics killed Mar 28
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and Hezbollah operations since March 2. 1,189 killed (124 children, 42 health workers), 3,229 injured per Lebanese MoH. ~1 million displaced (~20% of population). IDF claims 800+ Hezbollah operatives killed including hundreds from elite Radwan Force. 3 journalists killed in Jezzine (incl. Al Manar TV's Ali Shuaib). 9 paramedics killed Mar 28. 4 hospitals and 50+ primary healthcare centers closed. IDF advancing toward Litani River with 3+ divisions. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / IDF / CNN / WHO.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4.2M+ displaced — Iran 3.2M (600K-1M households) — Lebanon ~1M — 3,200 ships (4% of global tonnage) idle — dual chokepoint threat
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. 4.2M+ total displaced. Iran: 600K-1M households (~3.2M people). Lebanon: ~1 million (~20% of population). ~3,200 ships (4% of global tonnage) remain idle in Persian Gulf; 500+ waiting outside. Houthi Bab al-Mandeb threat creates dual chokepoint closure scenario. OCHA activated contingency plans across Iran, Afghanistan, Pakistan, Lebanon, Syria, OPT, and Yemen. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / IMO.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — 3,200 ships idle (4% global tonnage) — 500+ waiting outside Gulf — dual chokepoint threat — Bab al-Mandeb closure 'among options'
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. ~3,200 ships (4% of global tonnage) remain idle in Persian Gulf. 500+ waiting outside. Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure would create unprecedented dual chokepoint scenario. Marine war risk insurance effectively unavailable — premiums 5-10% of hull value. VLCC rates peaked $519,104/day. Air raid sirens in Bahrain/Kuwait Mar 29. Source: IMO / IATA / Lloyd's List / S&P Global.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: Iran 231 (Health Ministry) + Lebanon 124 + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — Minab school: 100+ schoolchildren — UNHRC investigating — 'toxic black rainfall' reported
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. Iran Health Ministry: 231 children among the dead. Lebanon: 124 children. Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. Minab school (Feb 28): 100+ schoolchildren aged 7-12 killed — UN Human Rights Council examining. WHO warned of 'toxic black rainfall' from oil infrastructure strikes. OCHA activated contingency plans across 7 countries. Source: UNICEF / Iran Health Ministry / Lebanese MoH / UNHRC / WHO.
